description_long: This is a rustic, wooden rack-style shelf, likely designed for holding long, narrow objects such as pool cues, umbrellas, or tools. The structure features a rectangular base with a raised back and two angled side supports, giving it a sturdy, open-frame appearance. Along the top rail, there are evenly spaced notches or slots, each capable of securely holding an individual item upright. The wood is visibly aged, with a weathered, stained finish and some dark patches, suggesting frequent use or exposure to the elements. The open design allows for easy access and visibility of stored items, making it suitable for garages, workshops, or public recreational spaces. There are no doors, drawers, or handles, emphasizing its utilitarian function as a storage and organization solution.
description: A weathered wooden rack-style shelf with notched slots for holding long, narrow items upright. Its open-frame design and rustic finish make it ideal for garages or recreational spaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the notched top rail and open storage area below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled support and thickness of the wooden panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid back panel and overall rectangular shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the angled support and wood grain details.
